legal.summary: Information acquired under the Act must be kept confidential; unauthorized disclosure is a criminal offence.

requirements.requirement: Ensure all persons performing functions under the Act maintain strict confidentiality of information acquired in the course of those functions
requirements.rationale: Breach of secrecy carries RM500,000/10 years — one of the highest penalties under the Act

requirements.requirement: Only disclose Act-related information for permitted purposes: purposes of the Act, civil/criminal proceedings, or with written CE consent
requirements.rationale: Three narrow exceptions define the boundaries of permitted disclosure

technical.summary: Technical controls for protecting Act-related information.

requirements.requirement: Implement information classification and handling procedures for Act-related data (incident reports, risk assessments, audit results, threat intelligence), with access restricted to authorized personnel
requirements.rationale: Technical controls must enforce the secrecy obligation across all systems and channels

requirements.requirement: Include Act secrecy obligations in staff training, employment contracts, and NDA arrangements for all personnel with access to Act-related information
requirements.rationale: Staff awareness and contractual commitments are the first line of defence against unauthorized disclosure

legal.summary: Directors and officers face personal criminal liability for corporate offences unless they prove due diligence. Corporate fines may be multiplied by 10x.

requirements.requirement: Directors, CEO, COO, managers and officers must be able to demonstrate that they exercised all due diligence to prevent the commission of any offence by the body corporate
requirements.rationale: Reverse burden of proof — officers are deemed to have committed the offence unless they can prove due diligence and lack of knowledge/consent/connivance

requirements.requirement: Maintain evidence of board-level engagement with NCII compliance, including meeting minutes, compliance reports reviewed, decisions made, and resources allocated
requirements.rationale: Due diligence defence requires demonstrable evidence of active governance, not passive awareness

technical.summary: No direct technical requirements; s52 is a liability provision.

governance.summary: Board governance framework to support due diligence defence.

requirements.requirement: Establish a Board Cyber Security Committee or assign NCII compliance oversight to an existing board committee with regular reporting cadence
requirements.rationale: Structured board oversight is the foundation of a credible due diligence defence under s52

requirements.requirement: Ensure adequate resources (budget, staffing, tools) are allocated for NCII compliance, with board-approved budget for cyber security risk treatment
requirements.rationale: Demonstrating resource allocation is key evidence of due diligence by directors
